etymology.description: semantic 頁(頁) (yè, "head") + phonetic 令(令) (lìng) -> related to the neck or collar
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 頁(頁) (yè, "head") suggests a connection to parts of the body close to the head, while the phonetic component 令(令) (lìng) provides the sound. This combination relates to the neck or collar, areas near the head.
etymology.simplification: Simplified by replacing 頁(頁) with 页 to reduce strokes.

examples.chinese: 他的衣服有一个黑色的领子(他的衣服有一個黑色的領子)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yīfú yǒu yīgè hēisè de lǐngzi.
examples.english: His shirt has a black collar.

examples.chinese: 她戴着一条珍珠项链在脖子上(她戴著一條珍珠項鏈在脖子上)，看起来非常优雅(看起來非常優雅)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dàizhe yītiáo zhēnzhū xiàngliàn zài bózi shàng, kànqǐlái fēicháng yōuyǎ.
examples.english: She is wearing a pearl necklace on her neck, and it looks very elegant.

examples.chinese: 这件衬衫的领特别舒服(這件襯衫的領特別舒服)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn chènshān de lǐng tèbié shūfú.
examples.english: The collar of this shirt is particularly comfortable.
